name: Melton Mowbray Pork Pie
recipeIngredient: 1 pinch Each of dry mustard and allspice
2/3 cup Milk mixed half and half with Water
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the meat, salt, pepper, herbs, and spices and set aside.
2. To make the stock, boil bones, onions, bay leaf, herbs, salt, and pepper in 4 cups of water for two hours or until the liquid is reduced to 2 1/3 cups. Cool, degrease, and refrigerate until it starts to jell.
3. To make the pastry, rub 2 oz of the lard into the flour mixed with a teaspoon of salt until it is the consistency of breadcrumbs. Boil the rest of the fat with the milk and water. Make a well in the mound of flour and mix in the boiling liquid while stirring with a wooden spoon. Knead and let rest for 10 minutes.
4. To make the casing or "coffyn", roll out three-quarters of the dough into a circle 1/4 inch thick. Place it over the meat mixture, shaping it around the tin, and gently remove the tin, leaving you with a pie casing.
5. Roll out the remaining dough into a circle slightly larger than the diameter of the casing to form the lid. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
6. Dampen the top edge of the pie and press on the lid. Crimp the edge. Make a hole in the center of the lid and decorate it with pastry leaves.
7. Place the pie on a baking dish and bake for 20 minutes at 400°F. Reduce the heat to 300°F and continue baking for 1 3/4 hours. If necessary, place aluminum foil on the top to prevent burning. Remove from the oven and let cool completely.
8. Pour the chilled stock through the hole in the lid and serve cold.
